title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
async/await is syntactic sugar over Promises, making asynchronous code look and feel like synchronous code.

• async function — always returns a Promise. You can await inside it.
• await — pauses execution of the async function until the Promise resolves/rejects. The rest of the program is NOT blocked.
• Error handling — wrap await calls in try/catch.
• Parallel execution — avoid sequential awaits when tasks are independent; use Promise.all instead.

await can only be used inside an async function (or at the top level of a module in modern environments).
